characters.description: A 22-year-old Illinois student, runner, and gamer who delayed college after his father's death. He agrees to live in Uncle Dan's shed so the conditional inheritance can relieve his family's debts and fund his and Cecilia's education.
characters.name: Silas Renner

characters.description: Silas and Cecilia's mother and the beneficiary of Uncle Dan's conditional will. She can receive the house, land, and trust if an eligible male relative lives in the shed, allowing the family to address its debts and education costs.
characters.name: Silas's mother

characters.description: Silas's college-bound sister, whom he calls Cease. The cost of her education is one reason he accepts the unusual residency condition attached to Uncle Dan's will, placing her future among the family obligations he intends to meet.
characters.name: Cecilia

characters.description: Silas's deceased uncle, whose will grants the family a house, land, and a trust if an eligible relative lives in the property's shed. He leaves Silas a key and a cryptic warning about the multiverse and people who depend on him.
characters.name: Daniel Becker

characters.description: A Norwegian man charged after an Oslo attack reportedly involved extraordinary destruction accomplished with his bare hands. The apparent superhuman incident is unexplained when his case first appears.
characters.name: Emil Larsson

characters.description: A member of the roster of Earth's active forerunners. At his introduction, the listing establishes his participation in the competition but gives Silas no direct knowledge of his abilities or intentions.
characters.name: Jiang Bai

characters.description: An active Earth forerunner listed alongside the other people contributing to Earth's standing. Silas initially knows her only through the induction roster, which offers no explanation of her abilities, history, or present assignment.
characters.name: Annika Patel

characters.description: One of the active Earth forerunners named on the induction roster. Her presence confirms that Silas is entering an existing group whose work affects Earth's survival, although he has not yet met her or learned what part she plays.
characters.name: Nuri Khalid

characters.description: The civic leader whose official signatory ring is the object of Silas's first off-world challenge. Silas is told to obtain the ring by any method, before he has learned anything about the official personally.
characters.name: Mayor of Anwich

characters.description: A level 17 rogue and mage who leads a visiting adventuring party into the Forest Den. She recognizes Silas as a forerunner candidate and invites him to join her group's attempt against the dungeon boss.
characters.name: Dory West

characters.description: Dory's heavily armored, hammer-wielding companion in the Forest Den party. He serves as the group's frontline fighter, joins the planned boss attempt, and is enthusiastic about working with a forerunner candidate.
characters.name: Crag

characters.description: The robed mage in Dory's Forest Den party. He joins Dory and Crag as one of Silas's temporary allies for a boss fight that the group considers too dangerous for Silas to attempt alone, adding magical support to their formation.
characters.name: Nevin

characters.description: A feared level 32 uncommon feathered bear that has roamed the forest near Anwich for more than two years. Local adventurers regard this mutated roaming monster as a threat beyond an ordinary dungeon creature.
characters.name: Greta

characters.description: A University of Illinois cheerleader who deliberately arranges an introduction to Silas on campus. Her approach immediately precedes his confrontation with Chad Williams and two other football players.
characters.name: April

characters.description: A University of Illinois football player who confronts Silas on campus alongside two teammates after April introduces herself to Silas.
characters.name: Chad Williams

characters.description: The university cross-country coach who rejects Silas from the team after his fight with several football players and warns that the incident could end his enrollment.
characters.name: Coach Wilson

characters.description: The university football coach who intervenes after Silas fights several players. He steers the group away from formal consequences and invites Silas to attend a football practice.
characters.name: Coach Johnson

characters.description: A level 26 ritual mage who admits Silas into the besieged city of Proximus. He identifies Silas as a heaven-blessed forerunner and offers protection and guidance while Silas learns about the city's magic.
characters.name: Malus Bunarn

characters.description: The leader of a captive Delmen company freed by Silas inside Proximus's defense complex. With the ritual chamber seized and its guards defeated, he becomes Silas's tactical ally and proposes destroying the city shield before reinforcements arrive.
characters.name: Faruk

characters.description: A figure Nuri names among Earth's other forerunners or candidates while warning Silas about the people connected to Earth's induction effort. No abilities or current assignment are established at this point.
characters.name: Morvog

characters.description: A defensive spirit companion summoned through Silas's inherited spell. It communicates with him mentally, shares beneficial magical effects, perceives its surroundings without ordinary sight, and chooses a form suited to protecting him.
characters.name: Eidolon

characters.description: A corporate operative on Galen who approaches under a distortion cloak and orders Silas to meet his superior. He is hostile toward Silas's local connections but has instructions not to harm him for the moment.
characters.name: Beck

characters.description: A university cheerleader who approaches Silas after his football tryout and asks him for a ride home.
characters.name: Chelsea

characters.description: A representative of an endangered, technologically advanced alien people. The envoy recalls dealings with Daniel Becker and offers knowledge and technology in return for possible future asylum on Earth.
characters.name: Gray alien envoy

characters.description: An elf-like rival forerunner whose powerful aura clouds attention and judgment. She arrives with a plantlike companion, condemns Earth's treatment of its environment, and openly threatens the Earth team before the race.
characters.name: Dana

characters.description: The name of Silas's Eidolon, revealed when the spirit begins speaking. Erg is a protective vine-and-shield companion mentally linked to Silas, not a nature elemental or an ordinary summoned creature, and he confronts a rival on Silas's behalf.
characters.name: Erg
